subject: Statistics
title: Interaction Effects
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Interaction Effects.

hook.text: Exercise reduces stress. Sleep reduces stress. But exercise plus good sleep reduces it way more than either alone.

buildup.text: An interaction means the effect of one variable depends on the level of another.

discovery.text: You model this by multiplying the two predictors together. The product term captures the combined effect.

twist.text: Ignoring interactions makes the model additive — each effect is fixed. Reality is usually more complex.

climax.text: Drug interactions, marketing synergies, and climate effects all require interaction terms to model correctly.

quiz.question: What is an interaction effect in regression?
options: When the effect of one predictor depends on the level of another predictor
When two predictors are highly correlated
When the model has more than two predictors
